data.title: 24-hour countdown to subscription: Gate Launchpad Phase 6 Immunefi (IMU) is about to conclude.
data.description: According to official news, Gate Launchpad's 6th Immunefi (IMU) subscription will end on January 21st at 16:00 (UTC+8), and spot trading of Immunefi (IMU) will commence on January 22nd at 22:00 (UTC+8). The total subscription amount for this round is 212,404,419 IMU, supporting both USD1 and GUSD subscriptions. The subscription price is 1 IMU = 0.01177 USD1/GUSD, with a maximum allocation of 2,124,044.2 IMU per user. Subscription is in full swing; the earlier you subscribe, the higher the returns. Seize the opportunity to get in early. In addition, Gate Savings Account is simultaneously launching USD1 investment benefits, allowing new users participating in IMU subscriptions to unlock an exclusive 200% annualized return.

data.title: Ethereum's Decline Expands Losses for Major Bitcoin Whale
data.description:
Ethereum's recent drop below $3,100 has significantly increased the unrealized losses for a major Bitcoin whale, according to ChainCatcher. The whale, known as 'BTC OG Insider Whale,' now faces an unrealized loss of $16.85 million. Previously, the only remaining profitable position in Solana has also turned into a loss.

The Bitcoin long position, leveraged at 5x, has an unrealized loss of $650,000. The Ethereum long position, also at 5x leverage, has an unrealized loss of $15.42 million. Meanwhile, the Solana long position, leveraged at 10x, has an unrealized loss of $780,000.

The cumulative funding fee loss amounts to $7.92 million, with the total position value standing at $848 million.

data.title: Future Competition Points for On-Chain Brokerages Amid Stock Tokenization
data.description:
Qinbafrank posted on X about the evolving landscape of stock tokenization as major exchanges like Nasdaq and NYSE enter the market. The prediction made in August last year regarding these exchanges potentially moving stocks from off-chain to on-chain has largely been validated. These exchanges, along with DTCC, are expected to facilitate the supply of stock tokens directly, eliminating the need for on-chain trading platforms to mint their own tokens. Instead, platforms can integrate these official tokens, transforming into 'new-type on-chain brokerages.'

The future competition for on-chain brokerages lies in the user-facing front end, as differentiation in the back end or supply side becomes less pronounced. Key competitive points include entering new markets, innovating user experience, and offering favorable rates. Brokerages may also provide users with various leverage models and innovate asset types and trading methods based on U.S. stock tokens, such as perpetual contracts, options, binary options, and collateralized lending, among other DeFi strategies. More innovative trading methods are likely to emerge in the future.

Additionally, there will be significant competition in terms of traffic and user engagement, where strategies from internet brokerages can be adapted. Ultimately, the goal is to become a 'super app,' offering a comprehensive platform for trading stocks, bonds, crypto assets, precious metals, agricultural products, commodities, and forex.

data.title: Concerns Raised Over ICO Management on HyperliquidX Platform
data.description: Darkfost posted on X. Concerns have been raised regarding the management of Initial Coin Offerings (ICOs) on the HyperliquidX platform. Users have suggested that HyperliquidX should improve its oversight of ICOs to ensure better outcomes. Comparisons have been made to JupiterExchange, which is perceived to be handling ICOs more effectively. The discussion highlights the importance of robust management practices in the cryptocurrency exchange sector.

data.title: Market Decline Leads to Partial Liquidation of Machi's Ethereum Position
data.description: Onchain Lens posted on X. Amidst a continuing market downturn, Machi, known by the handle @machibigbrother, experienced a partial liquidation of his Ethereum long position, which was leveraged at 25 times. The next potential liquidation is just $30 away. Currently, Machi faces a loss of $23.6 million, with a floating loss exceeding $1 million.

data.title: Market Volatility Concerns Raised by Blockchain Analyst
data.description: Blockchain sleuth EmberCN posted on X, highlighting concerns about market volatility. The analyst noted that while rolling positions can lead to quick gains, they also carry significant risks. A slight reversal in market direction could result in substantial losses, erasing both principal and profits. This observation underscores the importance of cautious trading strategies in volatile markets.

data.title: Ethereum Faces Repeated Resistance, Future Movement Uncertain
data.description: CyrilXBT posted on X. Ethereum (ETH) has encountered resistance at the same price level for the second time, raising questions about its future trajectory. If ETH fails to break through this resistance on its third attempt, it may continue to trade within the current range for the upcoming months. The cryptocurrency market is closely monitoring these developments to assess potential impacts on Ethereum's price movement.

data.title: Zcash Faces Bearish Market Amid Macro Challenges
data.description: Zcash (ZEC) experienced a significant price decline following an unsuccessful breakout attempt. According to NS3.AI, the downturn is attributed to deteriorating macroeconomic conditions and intensified selling pressure. Despite efforts by large investors to accumulate ZEC, the market remains bearish, as evidenced by rising exchange balances and negative funding rates, which suggest a prevalence of short positions. Technical analysis indicates a possible 55% decrease in value, potentially reaching $171, unless buyers can reestablish control above crucial support levels near $340.

data.title: Pundi AI and Bella Protocol Integrate AI Trading Signals
data.description: Pundi AI and Bella Protocol have successfully integrated their technologies, combining Bella's AI trading signals and market research tools with Pundi AI's decentralized data infrastructure. According to NS3.AI, this collaboration enables access to the Bella Signal Bot API, which offers real-time perpetual trade signals for specific token pairs and multi-position indicators. This integration supports developers and AI agents in exploring trading strategies. Additionally, the partnership includes the Bella Research Bot, a language model-based Telegram assistant that provides updates related to Pundi AI. This initiative aims to enhance transparent and accessible on-chain applications, furthering innovation in Web3 AI.

data.title: Understanding Twitter's Algorithm: Key Insights and Implications
data.description:
0xTodd posted on X. Elon Musk has fulfilled his promise to open-source Twitter's algorithm, providing users with insights into how content is curated and displayed on the platform. This move allows users to better understand what content they are likely to see and how they can effectively express their viewpoints to reach a wider audience.

The algorithm introduces two key concepts: 'Thunder,' representing accounts a user follows, and 'Phoenix Retrieval,' encompassing all Twitter content, including accounts not followed by the user. Additionally, each user has a hidden 'Grok' assistant that predicts engagement with posts and determines whether to display content from Thunder or Phoenix Retrieval.

Twitter's algorithm has evolved from hard-coded rules to a more dynamic system. For instance, in 2023, posts with videos were scored higher, while those with links were penalized. Now, the scoring is less mechanical, focusing on user preferences.

Several overlooked rules impact content visibility. An invisible reputation score, ranging from -128 to +100, is assigned to each user. New accounts start with a low score, limiting their content's reach until they interact positively with high-reputation users. Engaging with low-quality accounts can negatively affect one's score.

The algorithm also addresses 'topic fatigue,' reducing the ranking of multiple posts from the same author within a short period. Repetitive content on popular topics is penalized, encouraging timely posting.

Negative feedback, such as muting or blocking, carries more weight than positive interactions like likes. Extreme behavior can lead to reduced visibility, as one block can outweigh numerous loyal followers.

Verified accounts (Blue V) are favored in the algorithm, making it easier for their content to be retrieved by Phoenix Retrieval. Unverified accounts struggle to reach the initial candidate pool unless their engagement grows rapidly.

Other factors include dwell time, which adds points even if users don't interact with posts, and candidate isolation, where each tweet is evaluated independently. The algorithm avoids recommending repetitive or outdated content and emphasizes the importance of video completion rates.

These insights provide a deeper understanding of Twitter's algorithm, inviting further discussion and exploration.
